code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a fluent single‑sentence description from a list of triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping of predicate to natural‑language phrase
    phrase_map = {
        "almaMater": "graduated from",
        "birthDate": "was born on",
        "birthName": "was born as",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "dateOfRetirement": "retired in",
        "occupation": "was a",
        "status": "has status",
        "timeInSpace": "spent",
        "mission": "participated in mission",
        "selectedByNasa": "was selected by NASA in",
        "deathDate": "died on",
        "nationality": "was a citizen of",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "served as chief of the astronaut office in",
        "title": "held the title",
        "ribbonAward": "received the ribbon award",
        "operator": "was operated by",
        "backupPilot": "had backup pilot",
        "commander": "had commander",
        "crewMembers": "had crew member",
        "representative": "was represented by",
        "alternativeName": "also known as",
        "awards": "received",
        "fossil": "has fossil",
        "senators": "has senators",
        "part": "is part of",
        "partsType": "has parts type",
        "higher": "is higher than",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "bird": "has bird",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "competeIn": "competes in",
        "president": "has president",
        "award": "received the award",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "gemstone": "has gemstone",
        "mascot": "has mascot",
        "cosparId": "has COSPAR ID",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset",
    }

    # Find the main subject (most frequent subject)
    subject_count = {}
    for t in triples:
        subject_count[t.subject] = subject_count.get(t.subject, 0) + 1

    main_subject = max(subject_count, key=subject_count.get)

    # Group triples by subject
    triples_by_subject: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in triples_by_subject:
            triples_by_subject[t.subject] = []
        triples_by_subject[t.subject].append(t)

    # Build main clause for the main subject
    main_triples = triples_by_subject.get(main_subject, [])
    main_parts = []
    other_subjects = [s for s in triples_by_subject.keys() if s != main_subject]

    for t in main_triples:
        phrase = phrase_map.get(t.predicate, t.predicate.replace("_", " "))
        main_parts.append(f"{phrase} {t.object}")

    # Build relative clauses for related subjects
    relative_clauses = []
    for subj in other_subjects:
        subj_triples = triples_by_subject[subj]
        subj_parts = []
        for t in subj_triples:
            phrase = phrase_map.get(t.predicate, t.predicate.replace("_", " "))
            subj_parts.append(f"{phrase} {t.object}")
        if subj_parts:
            relative_clauses.append(f"{subj} {'; '.join(subj_parts)}")

    # Assemble final sentence
    main_clause = f"{main_subject} {'; '.join(main_parts)}"
    if relative_clauses:
        sentence = main_clause + "; " + ", ".join(relative_clauses)
    else:
        sentence = main_clause

    sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
